title: Roasted Sweet Potatoes & Beluga Lentils
subtitle: with Citrus Pecan Salad & Chipotle Crema
ingredients: 2 oz Treeline® Soft French-Style Cashew Cheese
2.5 oz Forager Project® Organic Dairy-Free Cashewmilk Yogurt

steps.text: Preheat the oven to 425°F. Rinse and sort the beluga lentils. Add lentils and 1¼ cups (2½ cups) water to a medium saucepan. Bring to a boil, cover, reduce heat to low, and simmer until lentils are tender and water is absorbed, 20 to 23 minutes. Drain any remaining water, transfer lentils to a bowl, and rinse the saucepan.

Halve sweet potatoes lengthwise, and cut each half into 3 to 4 long wedges. Transfer to a baking sheet cut side down, coat with 1 tbsp (2 tbsp) olive oil, and sprinkle with salt and pepper. Roast until tender, 22 to 26 minutes.

Peel the oranges and chop into bite-size pieces. Pick cilantro leaves and reserve the tender stems. Add chopped orange, cilantro leaves and stems, pecans, and a pinch of salt to a medium bowl, and stir to combine. Crumble cashew cheese into the bowl, and gently toss the citrus pecan salad. Peel and mince the garlic.
In a small bowl, combine the yogurt and ⅛ tsp (¼ tsp) chipotle morita spice. Stir the chipotle crema.

Return the medium saucepan to medium heat with 1 tbsp (2 tbsp) olive oil. Add minced garlic and cook until golden and toasted, 2 to 3 minutes. Add cooked lentils and cook until heated through. Taste, and add salt as necessary.
Slightly mash the sweet potatoes with a fork. Smear chipotle crema on large plates. Top with roasted sweet potatoes, beluga lentils and citrus pecan salad. Drizzle with olive oil. Bon appétit!

tips: TIP: We will use this saucepan again
TIP: Add more chipotle morita spice if you prefer.
This item is spicy. (chipotle morita spice)
